title: Dark Molten Jamaican Chicken Curry
description: A spicy and adventurous Jamaican dish featuring unsweetened cocoa, this dark molten chicken curry is a unique twist on traditional curry recipes, served in a lacquerware bowl.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the curry powder, unsweetened cocoa powder, and diced scotch bonnet peppers to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Cook for 1 minute, until the spices are fragrant.
Deglaze the skillet with the chicken broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the curry, covered, for 20 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the coconut milk and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the sauce is smooth and creamy.
Serve the curry hot, in a lacquerware bowl, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
